chapter_question: Why did a geometric account of relativity need parallel transport?
inspection_targets: The historical gap points from tensor calculus to transport.
Dates become a dependency chain, not a memorized story.
Curvature interpretation requires comparing tangent spaces.
Different tangent planes cannot be compared without a rule.

source_span.focus: Motivates parallel transport through the historical gap between Einstein's 1915 field equation and Levi-Civita's 1917 concept.
source_span.label: Chapter 21
source_span.title: An Historical Puzzle
topics: Parallel transport as a late foundational concept
Einstein's reconciliation of gravity and relativity
Tensor calculus as the available 1915 tool
Why a geometric interpretation needs parallel transport
